title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Sinusitis
intro: Herbal teas for Sinusitis are a type of medicinal tea made from various plants and herbs that are used to alleviate the symptoms of sinusitis, an inflammation or infection of the sinuses. These teas can treat sinusitis by reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and fighting off infections. Some examples of herbal teas that can treat sinusitis include ginger tea, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain; peppermint tea, which helps to clear congestion and ease sinus pressure; eucalyptus tea, which helps to reduce inflammation and fight off infections; and thyme tea, which has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat bacterial infections. Additionally, other herbal teas such as echinacea, slippery elm, and yarrow tea can also be used to treat sinusitis by boosting the immune system, reducing inflammation, and soothing the mucous membranes.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas, made from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis, an inflammatory condition affecting the sinuses. The herbal preparation's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties help to treat this ailment by reducing nasal congestion and swelling. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, particularly eucalyptol, contribute to its therapeutic effects by breaking down mucus, reducing inflammation, and opening up airways. Regular consumption of eucalyptus globulus teas can provide relief from sinusitis symptoms, such as headaches, facial pain, and difficulty breathing, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and add them to a large cup or teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a separate cup to remove the leaves and enjoy hot or let it cool.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Eucalyptus globulus tea a day to help relieve sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM", Eucalyptus globulus teas for sinusitis show potential therapeutic applications due to its ranking as the top-cited plant species used in the treatment of respiratory tract infections, including sinusitis, in Ethiopia.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can help alleviate sinusitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and congestion, but they may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and dizziness due to the high eucalyptol content. To use Eucalyptus globulus teas safely for sinusitis treatment, it is essential to start with low dosages, avoid drinking them on an empty stomach, and monitor your body's response to potential allergic reactions or interactions with other medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 3 times a day.
Take 2-3 teaspoons of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes before straining the tea.
Use Eucalyptus globulus tea for 7-14 days to relieve sinusitis symptoms.
Consult a doctor if sinusitis symptoms persist after 14 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been used for centuries to treat various health conditions, including sinusitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and alleviate congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities that help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the sinuses. Regular consumption of Zingiber officinale teas has been found to provide relief from sinusitis symptoms, such as headaches, facial pain, and nasal discharge, and may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of sinus infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger root, and a tea infuser.
Peel the ginger root and slice it into small pieces.
Place the ginger pieces in the tea infuser and put it in a pot or cup.
Bring the water to a boil and pour it over the ginger in the infuser.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Turkish archives of otorhinolaryngology", Zingiber officinale teas for sinusitis may be an effective treatment option as the herbal preparation Sinulan forte, which contains extracts of Zingiber officinale, showed significant improvement in symptoms and quality of life in patients with acute postviral rhinosinusitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can help alleviate sinusitis symptoms, but may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive stomachs. Precautions to take when using Zingiber officinale teas to treat sinusitis include starting with small doses to assess tolerance, avoiding consumption before bedtime due to potential drowsiness, and being cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to possible dizziness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of Zingiber officinale powder 2 times a day.
Use 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale oil in a vaporizer 2 times a day.
Inhale the vapor for 5 minutes each time.
Continue for 7-10 days or as advised by a doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been widely used to treat sinusitis, a common respiratory condition characterized by inflammation and infection of the sinuses. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of these teas help to reduce swelling and fight off bacterial and viral infections that cause sinusitis. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, present in Echinacea purpurea teas, contribute to their therapeutic effects by modulating the immune response and reducing oxidative stress. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea teas can provide relief from sinusitis symptoms, such as congestion, headaches, and fatigue, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers. If using fresh flowers, choose ones with bright purple color and no signs of wilting.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Echinacea purpurea flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop. Let it cool for 1-2 minutes to prevent scalding.
Pour the boiled water over the Echinacea flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea if using loose flowers.
Drink the Echinacea tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, Echinacea purpurea teas may have potential as an adjuvant symptomatic therapy in respiratory conditions, including sinusitis, due to its bioactive compounds that have diverse therapeutic applications.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat sinusitis, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching, as well as stomach upset and nausea due to its potential to interact with other medications. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, individuals should be cautious when consuming it in large quantities or for extended periods, as it may cause an overactive immune system response, and also avoid consuming it if they have any underlying medical conditions, such as liver or kidney disease.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea in 1 cup boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1-2 cups of Echinacea tea 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a doctor.
Use Echinacea tea for 7-10 days for relief from sinusitis symptoms.
Combine Echinacea tea with other treatments like saline rinse or steam inhalation for better results.
Consult a doctor before using Echinacea tea for extended periods or if symptoms persist.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, derived from the leaves of the thyme plant, have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis, an inflammatory condition affecting the nasal passages and sinuses. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of Thymus vulgaris teas help to reduce the severity of sinusitis symptoms by combating the underlying bacterial infections and inflammation. The bioactive constituents, including thymol, carvacrol, and rosmarinic acid, contribute to the therapeutic effects of Thymus vulgaris teas by exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Regular consumption of Thymus vulgaris teas has been found to provide relief from sinusitis symptoms, such as congestion, headaches, and facial pain, by promoting sinus drainage and reducing inflammation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh thyme leaves (Thymus vulgaris) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the thyme leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup to remove the thyme leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea to sweeten it.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Ceska a Slovenska farmacie : casopis Ceske farmaceuticke spolecnosti a Slovenske farmaceuticke spolecnosti", Thymus vulgaris teas can be used for sinusitis due to their antibacterial and antiseptic effects, which help in the treatment of respiratory tract diseases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating sinusitis, but it may cause side effects such as dry mouth, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to use Thymus vulgaris teas in moderation, avoid mixing it with other medications or herbal supplements, and monitor your body's response to this herbal preparation.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 3 times a day
Use 2-3 teaspoons of dried Thymus vulgaris per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds
Drink tea for 7-10 days to alleviate sinusitis symptoms
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage or frequency as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis, an inflammation of the sinuses, due to their potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing congestion and promoting the drainage of mucus, thereby alleviating symptoms such as facial pain and pressure. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis teas, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to their therapeutic effects. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat sinusitis include improved respiratory function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 tablespoon of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves, and a tea strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the tea strainer.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the leaves to release their flavor and oils.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Turkish archives of otorhinolaryngology, Rosmarinus officinalis teas, as part of the herbal preparation Sinulan forte, showed significant improvement in symptoms of sinusitis, such as nasal congestion and facial pain, and was found to be a safe and effective treatment for acute postviral rhinosinusitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be an effective treatment for sinusitis, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with a history of allergies or sensitivities. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with a small dose, gradually increase the amount, and be cautious when consuming this herbal preparation if you have high blood pressure, as it may interact with certain medications and exacerbate this condition.
preparation_usage: Boil 8-10 cups of water to prepare the tea.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, 2-3 times a week.
Use the tea for 2-4 weeks to see improvement in sinusitis symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation and fighting off bacterial infections that cause sinusitis. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, contribute to its therapeutic effects by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The benefits of this herbal preparation include relieving sinus pressure, reducing nasal congestion, and promoting a healthy respiratory system.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it's steaming hot.
Add 2 tablespoons of the Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the hot water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a doctor, to help relieve sinusitis symptoms.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat sinusitis, but its consumption may lead to side effects such as drowsiness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, as it may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and exacerbate conditions like low blood pressure, so it is recommended to start with low doses and monitor its effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea, 2-3 times daily.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried flowers to 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and drinking.
Continue for 7-10 days to alleviate sinusitis symptoms.
Consult a doctor for guidance on extended usage.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been widely used to treat sinusitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure. The bioactive constituents present in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to modulate the immune system and improve blood circulation, thereby helping to treat sinusitis. The benefits of Ginkgo biloba teas in treating sinusitis include their ability to thin mucus, reduce inflammation, and prevent the formation of bacterial biofilms, making them an effective natural remedy for this condition. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas has been found to provide long-term relief from sinusitis symptoms, making them a popular choice among individuals seeking a natural and holistic approach to managing this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of water, 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and a tea infuser or a small strainer.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add the Ginkgo biloba leaves to the tea infuser or strainer and place it in the pot.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then remove the leaves from the water.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool before drinking. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used as a natural remedy to help alleviate sinusitis symptoms, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, it's recommended to start with a small dose, monitor your blood pressure and blood sugar levels, and avoid using it if you have bleeding disorders or are taking anticoagulant medications.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Ginkgo biloba teas for sinusitis.
Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba per cup of tea.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 tablespoons per cup if needed.
Use Ginkgo biloba teas for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis, a common respiratory condition characterized by inflammation and congestion of the nasal passages and sinuses. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce swelling and fight off infections that contribute to sinusitis. The antioxidant properties of Sambucus nigra teas also help to soothe the mucous membranes and promote drainage, relieving congestion and sinus pressure. The benefits of using Sambucus nigra teas to treat sinusitis include reduced symptoms, improved respiratory function, and a quicker recovery time.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Sambucus nigra flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Sambucus nigra flowers in boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea if desired, and stir well.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Sambucus nigra tea 2-3 times a day as needed.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can help alleviate symptoms of sinusitis, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its high content of glycosides and tannins. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, while also avoiding consumption of Sambucus nigra teas with other medications, such as blood thinners, and being cautious when taking it with other herbal preparations that may have similar effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis due to their decongestant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of peppermint tea, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, help to alleviate sinusitis symptoms by reducing inflammation, relieving nasal congestion, and killing bacteria that cause infections. Drinking peppermint tea can help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and providing relief from sinus pressure and headaches associated with sinusitis. The benefits of peppermint tea in treating sinusitis include rapid relief from symptoms, reduced need for antibiotics, and improved overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh peppermint leaves (Mentha x piperita) or 2 teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and add the peppermint leaves to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture into a cup.
Drink the tea hot or let it cool, and repeat 3-4 times a day as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to BioMed research international, Mentha x piperita teas for sinusitis have significant potential therapeutic activity, as it was listed among the plants with higher use value in the study.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to treat sinusitis, but possible side effects include stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions such as hives or itching, especially in individuals with sensitive skin or digestive systems. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and be cautious when combining it with other medications or herbs that may interact with its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis due to their decongestant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce nasal congestion, inflammation, and bacterial or viral infections that cause sinusitis, thereby alleviating symptoms such as headaches, facial pain, and difficulty breathing. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, exhibit their therapeutic effects by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms, modulating the immune system, and reducing inflammation in the nasal passages. Regular consumption of Salvia officinalis teas may help to provide relief from sinusitis symptoms, improve respiratory function, and promote overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves, 2 cups of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Rinse the leaves with cold water, then chop them finely.
Combine the chopped leaves and water in a saucepan, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and let simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 2-3 cups a day to help relieve sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Salvia officinalis teas for sinusitis may be beneficial due to its extract reverting resistance to gentamicin in Staphylococcus aureus.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat sinusitis, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Salvia officinalis teas to treat sinusitis, it is essential to use them in moderation and be cautious of their potential interactions with other medications, as well as to avoid consuming them in large quantities or for extended periods to minimize the risk of adverse effects.

Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to sweeten it.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help relieve sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, there is no direct mention of using Piper nigrum teas for sinusitis, however, it's part of the Aller-7/NR-A2 polyherbal formulation that may serve as a potential treatment for allergic rhinitis, which can be associated with sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Piper nigrum teas can be used to treat sinusitis, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners, which can increase the risk of bleeding. To use this herbal preparation safely, it's recommended to start with a small dose and monitor for any adverse reactions, avoid consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and be cautious when combining it with other herbal remedies or certain foods.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis, an inflammation of the sinuses, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea angustifolia, including alkylamides, glycosides, and polyphenols, help to reduce inflammation and combat infections in the sinuses. By acting as a natural expectorant and mucolytic agent, Echinacea angustifolia teas help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, relieving congestion and sinus pressure. The benefits of using Echinacea angustifolia teas to treat sinusitis include reduced symptoms, improved respiratory function, and a lower risk of complications, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea angustifolia root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Echinacea root in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Echinacea root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey to taste, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day to help relieve sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Archives of Family Medicine, Echinacea angustifolia teas may have a potential relative risk reduction of 10% to 20% in preventing upper respiratory tract infections, although this effect has not been conclusively proven due to the small sample size in the study.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can be used to treat sinusitis, but its use may be associated with side effects such as nausea, stomach discomfort, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with low dosages, avoid consumption for extended periods, and be cautious when combining with other medications, especially blood thinners and immunosuppressants.

preparations.herb_desc: Sideritis scardica teas have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis, an inflammatory condition affecting the sinuses. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating symptoms of sinusitis. The bioactive constituents of Sideritis scardica, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects, which include reducing congestion, pain, and fever associated with sinusitis. By utilizing Sideritis scardica teas, individuals can potentially benefit from a natural, non-invasive treatment option that promotes sinus health and reduces the need for antibiotics and other medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Sideritis scardica flowers or 2 teaspoons of dried flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add the Sideritis scardica flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Daru : journal of Faculty of Pharmacy, Tehran University of Medical Sciences, Sideritis scardica teas have been traditionally used by local people for treatment of respiratory diseases, which may include sinusitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sideritis scardica teas can be used to treat sinusitis, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Sideritis scardica teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to the potential impact on fetal or infant development.

preparations.herb_desc: Ephedra sinica teas have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis, an inflammatory condition affecting the nasal passages and sinuses. The anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties of Ephedra sinica teas help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, providing relief from sinus pressure and pain. The bioactive constituents, including ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, act as bronchodilators, helping to open up airways and improve breathing, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties that combat underlying infections. By using Ephedra sinica teas, individuals can benefit from reduced sinus pressure, improved respiratory function, and a faster recovery from sinusitis.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Ephedra sinica leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Ephedra sinica leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add honey to taste, if desired, to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Ephedra sinica teas are associated with significant adverse effects, usually in the form of a hypersensitivity reaction, for the treatment of sinusitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ephedra sinica teas can be used to treat sinusitis, but possible side effects may include increased heart rate, high blood pressure, insomnia, and digestive issues such as stomach upset and diarrhea. Precautions when using Ephedra sinica teas for sinusitis treatment include avoiding use in individuals with pre-existing heart conditions, pregnancy, or breastfeeding, and limiting dosage to avoid excessive stimulation, as well as monitoring for any signs of allergic reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Trachyspermum ammi teas, also known as ajwain tea, have been traditionally used to treat sinusitis due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure. The bioactive constituents of Trachyspermum ammi, including thymol and carvacrol, exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities that help to combat bacterial and fungal infections, as well as soothe the mucous membranes. Drinking Trachyspermum ammi tea may help to clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby providing relief from sinusitis symptoms. The benefits of Trachyspermum ammi tea in treating sinusitis include its ability to reduce sinus pressure, alleviate headaches, and promote a sense of clearness and comfort in the nasal passages.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of Trachyspermum ammi seeds, and a tea strainer or infuser.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils.
Add the Trachyspermum ammi seeds to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea strainer or infuser.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, ideally 2-3 times a day for relief from sinusitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Trachyspermum ammi teas can be effective in treating sinusitis, but its use may lead to side effects such as allergic reactions, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To use Trachyspermum ammi teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and to discontinue use if any adverse reactions occur, such as dizziness or diarrhea.

preparation_study: According to Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, Eucalyptus globulus teas for sinusitis were not directly discussed in the given study, however, Eucalyptus globulus was one of the top-cited plant species used in the treatment of respiratory tract infections, specifically respiratory disorders, which may include sinusitis, at a frequency of 6.8% in Ethiopia.
intro_study: According to "Turkish archives of otorhinolaryngology", teas for sinusitis may be a viable option as an herbal product such as Sinulan forte, containing extracts of five medicinal plants, has been identified as a safe and effective treatment for acute postviral rhinosinusitis.
